content.problemStatement:
You once again find yourself exploring the beautiful region of Chefland.

There are NNN cities in a row. Each city has a color preference of CiC_iCi‚Äã and has a wealth of WiW_iWi‚Äã. Initially, you are at city 111 with a color of 000, and you have 000 coins. For simplicity's sake, it is guaranteed that C1=W1=0C_1=W_1=0C1‚Äã=W1‚Äã=0. Then, you may perform any of the following operations:


Set your color to any integer you wish. This reduces the amount of coins you have by KKK.
Move from city iii to city i+1i + 1i+1. If your current color is the same as Ci+1C_{i + 1}Ci+1‚Äã, you will gain Wi+1W_{i + 1}Wi+1‚Äã coins. Otherwise, you will lose Wi+1W_{i + 1}Wi+1‚Äã coins.
If you are at city NNN, your travel ends.
Note that you can have a negative amount of coins at any time.

What is the maximum number of coins you can end up with after this trip?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK, the number of cities and the cost to change colour.
The second line contains NNN integers - W1,W2,‚Ä¶,WNW_1, W_2, \ldots, W_NW1‚Äã,W2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,WN‚Äã.
The third line contains NNN integers - C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer, the maximum number of coins you can have after ending your travel.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: It is optimal to never change your color. You will lose 222 coins from entering city 222 with the wrong color and lose 444 coins for entering city 333 with the wrong color.
Test Case 2: It is optimal to change your color to 111. It costs 333 coins to change your color, and you will gain 666 coins from entering cities 222 and 333.
